channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: GhazalBench: Usage-Grounded Evaluation of LLMs on Persian Ghazals
item.description: arXiv:2603.09979v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Persian poetry plays an active role in Iranian cultural practice, where verses by canonical poets such as Hafez are frequently quoted, paraphrased, or completed from partial cues. Supporting such interactions requires language models to engage not onl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, framework, large language model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Large Language Models and Book Summarization: Reading or Remembering, Which Is Better?
item.description: arXiv:2603.09981v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Summarization is a core task in Natural Language Processing (NLP). Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) and the introduction of large context windows reaching millions of tokens make it possible to process entire books in a single prompt. A...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, context, arxiv, context window, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AraModernBERT: Transtokenized Initialization and Long-Context Encoder Modeling for Arabic
item.description: arXiv:2603.09982v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Encoder-only transformer models remain widely used for discriminative NLP tasks, yet recent architectural advances have largely focused on English. In this work, we present AraModernBERT, an adaptation of the ModernBERT encoder architecture to Arabic,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, transformer, context, arxiv, embedding | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Dunning-Kruger Effect in Large Language Models: An Empirical Study of Confidence Calibration
item.description: arXiv:2603.09985v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse tasks, yet their ability to accurately assess their own confidence remains poorly understood. We present an empirical study investigating whether LLMs exhibit patter...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, experiment, study, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evolving Demonstration Optimization for Chain-of-Thought Feature Transformation
item.description: arXiv:2603.09987v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Feature Transformation (FT) is a core data-centric AI task that improves feature space quality to advance downstream predictive performance. However, discovering effective transformations remains challenging due to the large space of feature-operator ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, context, library, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Causally Grounded Mechanistic Interpretability for LLMs with Faithful Natural-Language Explanations
item.description: arXiv:2603.09988v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mechanistic interpretability identifies internal circuits responsible for model behaviors, yet translating these findings into human-understandable explanations remains an open problem. We present a pipeline that bridges circuit-level analysis and nat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, model, template, arxiv, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The System Hallucination Scale (SHS): A Minimal yet Effective Human-Centered Instrument for Evaluating Hallucination-Related Behavior in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.09989v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce the System Hallucination Scale (SHS), a lightweight and human-centered measurement instrument for assessing hallucination-related behavior in large language models (LLMs). Inspired by established psychometric tools such as the System Usab...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, tool, model, API, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Two-Stage Architecture for NDA Analysis: LLM-based Segmentation and Transformer-based Clause Classification
item.description: arXiv:2603.09990v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In business-to-business relations, it is common to establish NonDisclosure Agreements (NDAs). However, these documents exhibit significant variation in format, structure, and writing style, making manual analysis slow and error-prone. We propose an ar...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, model, transformer, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory for GUI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.10291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The remarkable progress of vision-language models (VLMs) has enabled GUI agents to interact with computers in a human-like manner. Yet real-world computer-use tasks remain difficult due to long-horizon workflows, diverse interfaces, and frequent inter...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, vision, arxiv, GPT, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Scalars: Evaluating and Understanding LLM Reasoning via Geometric Progress and Stability
item.description: arXiv:2603.10384v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating LLM reliability via scalar probabilities often fails to capture the structural dynamics of reasoning. We introduce TRACED, a framework that assesses reasoning quality through theoretically grounded geometric kinematics. By decomposing reaso...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, arxiv, reasoning, framework, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Verbalizing LLM's Higher-order Uncertainty via Imprecise Probabilities
item.description: arXiv:2603.10396v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the growing demand for eliciting uncertainty from large language models (LLMs), empirical evidence suggests that LLM behavior is not always adequately captured by the elicitation techniques developed under the classical probabilistic uncertain...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, context, arxiv, prompting, in-context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Resource-constrained Amazons chess decision framework integrating large language models and graph attention
item.description: arXiv:2603.10512v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Artificial intelligence has advanced significantly through the development of intelligent game-playing systems, providing rigorous testbeds for decision-making, strategic planning, and adaptive learning. However, resource-constrained environments pose...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, vision, arxiv, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: IH-Challenge: A Training Dataset to Improve Instruction Hierarchy on Frontier LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.10521v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Instruction hierarchy (IH) defines how LLMs prioritize system, developer, user, and tool instructions under conflict, providing a concrete, trust-ordered policy for resolving instruction conflicts. IH is key to defending against jailbreaks, system pro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, RAG, tool, model, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Adaptive RAN Slicing Control via Reward-Free Self-Finetuning Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.10564v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The integration of Generative AI models into AI-native network systems offers a transformative path toward achieving autonomous and adaptive control. However, the application of such models to continuous control tasks is impeded by intrinsic architect...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, context, fine-tuning, arxiv, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Does LLM Alignment Really Need Diversity? An Empirical Study of Adapting RLVR Methods for Moral Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.10588v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has achieved remarkable success in logical reasoning tasks, yet whether large language model (LLM) alignment requires fundamentally different approaches remains unclear. Given the apparent toleranc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, reasoning, study, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Explainable LLM Unlearning Through Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.09980v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM unlearning is essential for mitigating safety, copyright, and privacy concerns in pre-trained large language models (LLMs). Compared to preference alignment, it offers a more explicit way by removing undesirable knowledge characterized by specific...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, augmented, model, arxiv, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MoE-SpAc: Efficient MoE Inference Based on Speculative Activation Utility in Heterogeneous Edge Scenarios
item.description: arXiv:2603.09983v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models enable scalable performance but face severe memory constraints on edge devices. Existing offloading strategies struggle with I/O bottlenecks due to the dynamic, low-information nature of autoregressive expert activation...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, arxiv, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Personalized Group Relative Policy Optimization for Heterogenous Preference Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2603.10009v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite their sophisticated general-purpose capabilities, Large Language Models (LLMs) often fail to align with diverse individual preferences because standard post-training methods, like Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF), optimize for...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, RLHF, arxiv, framework, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LWM-Temporal: Sparse Spatio-Temporal Attention for Wireless Channel Representation Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.10024v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LWM-Temporal is a new member of the Large Wireless Models (LWM) family that targets the spatiotemporal nature of wireless channels. Designed as a task-agnostic foundation model, LWM-Temporal learns universal channel embeddings that capture mobility-in...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, fine-tuning, arxiv, experiment, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Revisiting Sharpness-Aware Minimization: A More Faithful and Effective Implementation
item.description: arXiv:2603.10048v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sharpness-Aware Minimization (SAM) enhances generalization by minimizing the maximum training loss within a predefined neighborhood around the parameters. However, its practical implementation approximates this as gradient ascent(s) followed by applyi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, RAG, arxiv, experiment, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: InFusionLayer: a CFA-based ensemble tool to generate new classifiers for learning and modeling
item.description: arXiv:2603.10049v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Ensemble learning is a well established body of methods for machine learning to enhance predictive performance by combining multiple algorithms/models. Combinatorial Fusion Analysis (CFA) has provided method and practice for combining multiple scoring...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, tool, analysis, model, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Cluster-Aware Attention-Based Deep Reinforcement Learning for Pickup and Delivery Problems
item.description: arXiv:2603.10053v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Pickup and Delivery Problem (PDP) is a fundamental and challenging variant of the Vehicle Routing Problem, characterized by tightly coupled pickup--delivery pairs, precedence constraints, and spatial layouts that often exhibit clustering. Existing...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, transformer, arxiv, ICL, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Training Language Models via Neural Cellular Automata
item.description: arXiv:2603.10055v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Pre-training is crucial for large language models (LLMs), as it is when most representations and capabilities are acquired. However, natural language pre-training has problems: high-quality text is finite, it contains human biases, and it entangles kn...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, reasoning, attention, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HTMuon: Improving Muon via Heavy-Tailed Spectral Correction
item.description: arXiv:2603.10067v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Muon has recently shown promising results in LLM training. In this work, we study how to further improve Muon. We argue that Muon's orthogonalized update rule suppresses the emergence of heavy-tailed weight spectra and over-emphasizes the training alo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, analysis, arxiv, experiment, image | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, prompt engineering, prompt, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, augmented, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, vector, reasoning, knowledge base | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, platform, framework, prompt, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: Quantifying Hallucinations in Language Language Models on Medical Textbooks
item.description: arXiv:2603.09986v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hallucinations, the tendency for large language models to provide responses with factually incorrect and unsupported claims, is a serious problem within natural language processing for which we do not yet have an effective solution to mitigate against...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, experiment, prompt, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HEAL: Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning for Reasoning Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2603.10359v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Distilling reasoning capabilities from Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) into smaller models is typically constrained by the limitation of rejection sampling. Standard methods treat the teacher as a static filter, discarding complex "corner-case" problems...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, reasoning, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CUAAudit: Meta-Evaluation of Vision-Language Models as Auditors of Autonomous Computer-Use Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.10577v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Computer-Use Agents (CUAs) are emerging as a new paradigm in human-computer interaction, enabling autonomous execution of tasks in desktop environment by perceiving high-level natural-language instructions. item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (Hugging Face Blog)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, tool, prompt engineering, prompt, step-by-step | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
